calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-12-2026 Continued To Oct-12-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs' Special Interrogatories Against Defendant Cristin Cummings And Their Counsel
Off Calendar Apr-27-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (by Zoom):
Rachel Neff, Esq. (866) 303-7267 THE LAW FIRM FOR TENANT RIGHTS, INC. for Plaintiff.
Darrell Nguyen, Esq. aaron Shaw, Esq. 415-625-9373 Wilson Elser Moskowitz Edelman & Dicker LLP for Defendant.

ARGUED.
The Court announces the tentative rulings, comments, poses questions.
Ms. Neff is not opposing.
Mr. Nguyen contests and presents arguments on the issue of sanctions, the motions are similar, requests to reduce amount.
Ms. Neff responds. Sanctions are appropriate, so many meet and confers and attempts to do so, prepared separate statments.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

2 - Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to the special interrogatories served on defendant Cristin Cummings is GRANTED IN PART and DENIED IN PART. The motion to compel further responses is DENIED as moot. Plaintiff's motion targets discovery responses served on January 30, 2026. (Neff Decl., par. 7.) On April 8, 2026, defendant served amended responses that supersede the prior responses. (Nguyen Decl., par. 13.) Per CRC 3.1348(a), the court awards $850 in sanctions. Defendant's delay in providing its responses lacked substantial justification. Defendant and counsel are jointly and severally liable for the sanctions and they shall be paid to plaintiff within 10 days of notice of entry of this order.

Ms. Neff is required to prepare proposed orders which repeat verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
